headline: Gulf monarchies divided amid threats of new US strikes on Iran
description: During a visit to the Gulf countries, French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot spoke to the divided allies, who seek to acheive greater autonomy over their own defense while avoiding upsetting Washington.

headline: Trump's withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany marks turning point in relations between the two countries
Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
Labor Day protesters in Paris denounce proposed changes to the paid holiday
description: According to the Interior Ministry, around 158,000 people took to the streets across France on Friday (more than 300,000, according to the CGT labor union), holding largely peaceful demonstrations.

headline: The Lebrun brothers and the French team's dream of a first world table tennis title is 'in the back of their minds'
description: With five players ranked in the world Top 30, the French national table tennis team is once again a strong contender for a medal at the World Team Championships, the final stage of which is taking place from May 2 to May 10 in London.

headline: Ruth Gruenthal, 103-year-old Holocaust survivor, reclaims her German citizenship
description: Eighty-six years after being stripped of her citizenship, the American retiree living in New York decided to have it 'restored' out of fear of Donald Trump's authoritarian tendencies.

headline: How Paris became the capital of master sushi chefs
description: In recent years, a small constellation of Michelin-starred 'sushiya' has appeared in the heart of Paris. In these temples of Japanese tradition, renowned sushi chefs have drawn inspiration from French cuisine and local French products to create remarkable and original flavors.

Russia drastically scales back May 9 parade over fears of Ukrainian drone attacks
description: For the first time since 2008, the victory parade will feature no military hardware. In recent months, Ukraine has repeatedly carried out drone strikes deep inside Russia.

headline: In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
description: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: Bardella's new economic advisor faces questions over his business track record
description: François Durvye has claimed to have doubled the far-right billionaire Pierre-Edouard Stérin's net worth when he managed his 'family office.' However, several elements have raised doubts over the credibility of his figures, as most of his growth projects never came to fruition.
